features.name: Gemini AI Grading Service
features.description: Connects to Gemini API to grade writing essays and speaking audio recordings based on rubrics.
features.evidence: backend/app/services/ai_grading.py — grade_writing()/grade_speaking() with safe mock fallback; verified by tests/test_specs_grading.py::test_SPEC_GRADE_004_ai_grading_degrades_safely (SPEC-GRADE-004 active). REAL-MODE SPEAKING FIX (C14): grade_speaking previously did httpx.get() on the stored relative path (/static/uploads/xxx.webm) which raises UnsupportedProtocol -> real-mode Speaking always scored 0 the moment GEMINI_API_KEY is set. Added _load_audio_bytes() that reads local /static paths straight from disk (robust on single-host Render — no self-HTTP/public-URL needed) while still fetching genuine http(s) URLs (remote storage); mime type now derived from the recording extension. Guarded by tests/test_specs_grading.py::test_grade_speaking_real_mode_loads_local_static_audio (FakeModel forces the real-mode branch + a real local file -> asserts the model receives the exact disk bytes and returns the parsed score; non-tautological — fails before the fix). Full suite 54 passed / 0 failed; ruff + architecture clean. C20 REAL-MODE NOW ACTUALLY WORKS (verified with a real key in backend/.env): while verifying the full VSTEP B1 exam, real grading surfaced TWO bugs — (1) the hardcoded model 'gemini-1.5-flash' was RETIRED by Google -> every real Writing/Speaking call returned 404; (2) a Speaking question submitted without a recording hit _load_audio_bytes(None) -> AttributeError. Fixes: model id is now config-driven (settings.GEMINI_MODEL, default 'gemini-2.5-flash' — one of 37 models the key lists; swappable via .env so a future retirement is a config change, not code); grade_speaking guards a missing audio_url -> returns score 0 + 'Không có bản ghi âm' gracefully (no crash). VERIFIED with the real Gemini key (local): submitted VSTEP B1 -> Writing graded by Gemini 2.5 (score 6.5 + specific real feedback 'The student's essay is remarkably clear, concise, and grammatically accurate...'), Speaking-without-audio -> 0.0 (no crash), MCQ 3/9; backend log shows no 404/AttributeError. Guard test tests/test_specs_grading.py::test_grade_speaking_without_audio_does_not_crash (FakeModel asserts model is NOT called when audio missing). Full suite 60/0/0. NOTE: LIVE/Render still mock (no key there) — set GEMINI_API_KEY on Render to enable real grading in production. S37 (24/06): SDK migrated off the EOL google-generativeai onto the supported google-genai (requirements pinned google-genai>=2.10.0,<3.0.0, resolved 2.10.0); ai_grading.py now uses genai.Client + types.GenerateContentConfig + types.Part.from_bytes (mock path + dict shapes preserved; alias self.model=self.client keeps the mock-mode monkeypatch tests unchanged; the two real-mode guards refactored to the new SDK shape with assertions intact). Claude independently verified: pytest 60/0 twice, ruff + architecture clean, EOL FutureWarning gone, grep confirms no old-SDK references; real-mode exercised with the local key (gemini-flash-latest returned a genuine score 4.0 + feedback + grammar_errors, proving the new client.models.generate_content + json.loads(response.text) path works end-to-end; default gemini-2.5-flash returns 503=busy not 404=retired). Anti executed on Dat (uncommitted), committed locally by Claude; push/deploy held for Đạt. S38 (24/06): default grading model bumped settings.GEMINI_MODEL 'gemini-2.5-flash' -> 'gemini-3.5-flash' (Đạt: use the newest/best flash). Verified the key supports it via client.models.list() (also exposes gemini-3-flash-preview / gemini-3.1-*); on the free tier 3.5-flash currently returns 503=busy (NOT 404=retired) so a real grade couldn't be pulled through at test time, but the guard/try-except returns 0 gracefully and it works once capacity frees up — override GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-flash-latest via env if 503 persists. Deployed (origin/main 3095396). S38-retry (SPEC-GRADE-005, branch feat/grading-retry): added _call_with_retry to ai_grading.py — exponential backoff + jitter on TRANSIENT Gemini errors (APIError.code 503/429, .status UNAVAILABLE/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, httpx timeout), fail-fast on PERMANENT errors (404/401/403 re-raised immediately), total sleep capped <=20s (grading runs inline in the submit request on Render free → avoid HTTP timeout); on retry exhaustion keeps the existing graceful score-0 + error-feedback path (no crash). config.py adds env-tunable GEMINI_MAX_RETRIES(4) + GEMINI_RETRY_BASE_DELAY(1.5). Keeps gemini-3.5-flash. Claude verified independently: test_SPEC_GRADE_005 is non-tautological — raises REAL google.genai.errors.ServerError/ClientError (so the .code/.status/.message transient-detection is checked against the actual SDK exception shape) across 4 cases (503x2->success/3 calls; 404->fail-fast/1 call/0; 429-forever->exactly 5 calls/0; large base_delay->cumulative sleep<=20s); pytest 63/0 x2, ruff/architecture clean, correct branch + no harness touched. Context (Đạt 25/06): after Đạt set GEMINI_API_KEY on Render, LIVE grading returned 503 (NOT mock -> key WORKS; it's free-tier + 3.5-flash saturation). MVP scaling direction = ~20-30 concurrent on free is enough, defer 500-1000 paid scaling to the very end (see [[mvp-scaling-decision]]). MODEL DECISION (25/06, after empirical load measurement): default GEMINI_MODEL reverted gemini-3.5-flash -> gemini-2.5-flash for the FREE MVP phase. Measured on real Gemini free tier: gemini-3.5-flash returns 503 'high demand' even for a SINGLE call (0/1), while gemini-2.5-flash grades 15/15 concurrent essays OK (~12s each with retry). Root-caused (client.models.get): NOT our bug — 3.5-flash name is valid (error is 503 not 404), our code is correct (2.5-flash works with identical code), it's that 3.5-flash (version 3.5-flash-05-2026, released ~1 month ago) gets near-zero FREE capacity from Google (reserved for paid). gemini-3.5-flash stays the target for when on a PAID tier (env-swappable GEMINI_MODEL, no code change). Deployed retry LIVE (origin/main 030d11d).
